jokes.text: Anniversary? You asked me to throw a Valentine's Day party.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions throwing an anniversary party for Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Lucille reveals she thought it was Valentine's Day
jokes.explanation: Classic Bluth family miscommunication. Efficient setup/payoff with character-specific humor about their dysfunction.

jokes.text: I deceived you, Mom. Tricked makes it sound like we have a playful relationship.
jokes.setup: Lucille says Lindsay tricked her
jokes.punchline: Lindsay corrects it to 'deceived' because it's more hostile
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Lindsay's passive-aggressive relationship with her mother. Brilliant word choice precision.

jokes.text: Yeah. Well, if I show up with all that stuff... I could probably just get away with shorts and a T-shirt, right?
jokes.setup: Lindsay describes romantic gesture with tuxedo and champagne
jokes.punchline: Michael deflates it by suggesting he could dress down if he brings fancy stuff
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's practical, unromantic nature through his misunderstanding of romantic gestures.

jokes.text: Which is good 'cause I don't even have a tuxedo.
jokes.setup: Michael just suggested dressing down for romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Reveals he couldn't dress up even if he wanted to
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation showing Michael's financial/romantic limitations, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: There was somebody for a while, but it was too much of a brother... bother.
jokes.setup: Michael asked about romantic interests
jokes.punchline: Near-Freudian slip revealing the 'brother' problem with Marta
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels - sounds like a slip but reveals the core conflict. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: That can't feel good.
jokes.setup: Michael's obvious verbal fumble about romantic trouble
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's understated response to his clear discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that acknowledges the awkwardness without overplaying it.

jokes.text: Well, if you call not filing for divorce 'something right.'
jokes.setup: Michael says 14 years means they're doing something right
jokes.punchline: Lindsay reveals the low bar for their marriage success
jokes.explanation: Sharp subversion of the 'successful marriage' expectation with dark but honest humor about their dysfunction.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa! There's still plenty of meat on that bone. You take this home, throw it in a pot. Add some broth, a potato... Baby, you got a stew goin'.
jokes.setup: Tobias asking about his acting career prospects
jokes.punchline: Carl treats the conversation like leftover food, literally talking about making stew
jokes.explanation: Iconic Carl Weathers moment that became a cultural reference. Perfect character absurdity mixing metaphors with literal cooking advice.

jokes.text: Never once touched my per diem. I go to craft service, get some raw veggies, bacon, cup of soup... [Chuckles] Baby, I had a stew goin'.
jokes.setup: Carl telling another acting story
jokes.punchline: Returns to the stew obsession again, showing it's his solution to everything
jokes.explanation: Immediate callback that establishes Carl's stew obsession as a character trait. Less original than first but builds the running gag.

jokes.text: I think I'd like my money back.
jokes.setup: Carl's completely off-topic stew advice instead of acting coaching
jokes.punchline: Tobias's restrained response to the obvious scam
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction. Tobias's understated response to the absurd situation.

jokes.text: Just give me a sign. [Chuckles] First one's obvious. Okay.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking for a sign about his feelings
jokes.punchline: Immediately sees a literal sign and dismisses it as too obvious
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the sign gag sequence. Shows George Michael's earnestness and overthinking.

jokes.text: 'Maybe tonight.' [Chuckling] What are the chances? [Narrator] Actually, 'Maybe Tonight' was a very popular candy slogan... and the chances were one in eight.
jokes.setup: George Michael thinks he got a miraculous sign
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the mundane statistical reality
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator deflation. Perfect subversion of romantic comedy 'signs' with cold statistical reality.

jokes.text: Just tryin' to throw stuff at my dad's head... But the wind keeps taking it.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks what Maeby is doing
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions violent intent toward her father, frustrated by weather
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby line - casually violent toward family with practical concerns about execution.

jokes.text: Mother, you know I have class on Tuesday. Oh, how silly of me.
jokes.setup: Lucille invites Carl to dinner when Tobias has class
jokes.punchline: Lucille's fake surprise reveals this was intentional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Shows Lucille's manipulative nature and sets up the Carl/Lucille situation with typical Bluth scheming.

jokes.text: Oh, well, I never say no to dinner, with a beautiful lady that is. Do you like ham? No. I love it.
jokes.setup: Carl agreeing to dinner and Lucille asking about ham
jokes.punchline: Carl's enthusiastic correction from 'like' to 'love' about ham
jokes.explanation: Shows Carl's enthusiasm for food and sets up his relationship with Lucille. Character-specific but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Good grief. He sent us one of his idiot videotapes.
jokes.setup: Family expecting anniversary toast, gets Caged Wisdom infomercial
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's blunt assessment of her father's self-help product
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction showing family's assessment of George Sr.'s schemes, though fairly straightforward.

jokes.text: You have got to learn to be alone. I cheated and I lied. And I whored around.
jokes.setup: George Sr. giving life advice in self-help video
jokes.punchline: Immediately confesses to terrible behavior contradicting his wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Sr. moment - dispensing advice while admitting to behavior that makes him unqualified to give it.

jokes.text: This was supposed to be my anniversary toast. Yeah. And this was supposed to be my Valentine's Day party.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about the video instead of toast
jokes.punchline: Lucille one-ups her with the party mix-up callback
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing both women's disappointment and competitive complaining.

jokes.text: [Lupe] You want balloons? Speech, speech, speech...
jokes.setup: Lucille asking about balloons, family chanting for speech
jokes.punchline: Lupe's practical question gets drowned out by meaningless chanting
jokes.explanation: Shows the family's dysfunction - getting caught up in mob mentality while ignoring practical concerns.

jokes.text: [Narrator] The family continued to chant, 'Speech, speech, speech'... for no one in particular.
jokes.setup: Family chanting for speech enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals they don't even know who should give the speech
jokes.explanation: Brilliant narrator observation that makes the mob mentality even more absurd - they're chanting with no purpose.

jokes.text: After all that, I was kinda hoping somebody would make a speech. Speech, speech, speech...
jokes.setup: Michael's reasonable expectation after all the chanting
jokes.punchline: Family immediately resumes chanting after his complaint
jokes.explanation: Shows the family's inability to listen or think - they just resume the chanting without processing Michael's point.

jokes.text: [Chuckling] Don't forget my girlfriend. That's who I meant.
jokes.setup: Michael toasting 'Buster and Lucille'
jokes.punchline: Buster has to clarify he meant Lucille 2, not his mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment highlighting Buster's Oedipal relationship and the confusion with two Lucilles.

jokes.text: I almost said George Michael and Maeby. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Michael listing couples at the anniversary party
jokes.punchline: Almost pairs up the cousins, laughs at his 'mistake'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Michael laughs at pairing the cousins while George Michael is secretly pining for exactly that.

jokes.text: I love you all, Marta.
jokes.setup: Toast about love and happiness concludes
jokes.punchline: Gob's grammatically confused declaration of love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment showing his narcissism and inability to express emotion properly.

jokes.text: I made a huge mistake.
jokes.setup: Marta realizes Michael's the right man after his speech
jokes.punchline: Uses the exact phrase associated with Gob's relationship regrets
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and dramatic irony - Marta has the same realization as Gob but about the opposite brother.

jokes.text: The man's psychologically incapable of taking all of his clothes off.
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining why she wants divorce
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tobias has never been fully naked
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for the never-nude revelation - specific, absurd, and character-defining all at once.

jokes.text: [Narrator] In fact, Tobias was a never-nude. As of yet, the D.S.M. - IV has not acknowledged the affliction.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's complaint about Tobias not getting naked
jokes.punchline: Narrator provides clinical context for the absurd condition
jokes.explanation: One of AD's most iconic moments. Creates entire running gag with clinical precision and absurd specificity.

jokes.text: On the other hand, I am doing it anyway... so let me suggest before you talk divorce, at least consider a separation.
jokes.setup: Michael says he's not one to judge Tobias's quirks
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he's judging anyway and gives advice
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment showing his self-awareness about being judgmental while doing exactly what he says he shouldn't.

jokes.text: I was gonna take her with me in the night. She's part of the bolt.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about Maeby in divorce plans
jokes.punchline: Lindsay casually includes daughter in her escape plan
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Lindsay's casual attitude toward taking her daughter in a midnight escape.

jokes.text: Yeah. I never want to look at Mom again.
jokes.setup: Buster praising Michael's speech about family and being true to yourself
jokes.punchline: Takes the message to mean cutting off his mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster interpretation of Michael's advice - taking family unity message to mean family estrangement.

jokes.text: Do you know she tried to set up my girlfriend with a man?
jokes.setup: Buster explaining his mother troubles
jokes.punchline: Reveals his specific complaint about mother's interference
jokes.explanation: Shows Buster's naive view of his mother's manipulation and his relationship with older woman.

jokes.text: I'm a man, Mike! I'm a man. Can I throw out this yogurt?
jokes.setup: Buster declaring his independence and manhood
jokes.punchline: Immediately interrupted by asking mom's permission for basic task
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Buster's character - passionate declaration of independence immediately undercut by dependency.

jokes.text: Secondly, you don't want to take any chances with yogurt, Mom. Just let her throw it out.
jokes.setup: Michael giving Buster life advice about moving out
jokes.punchline: Switches to practical yogurt safety advice for mom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - trying to handle family crisis while also giving practical food safety advice.

jokes.text: Wouldn't let me make love to her on Mom's bed. I don't even want to tell you what she wouldn't let me do to her in the car.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining Marta's strange behavior as proof of cheating
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate location preferences and oversharing
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob oversharing and inappropriate behavior, showing his narcissism and crudeness.

jokes.text: She kept using this guy's name, like, 'Hermano.'
jokes.setup: Gob suspicious about Marta's phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Doesn't realize 'hermano' means 'brother' in Spanish
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony setup - Gob's jealousy about 'hermano' when it literally means brother and Michael is the actual threat.

jokes.text: Let me tell you something. We're gonna track this 'Hermano' down, and we're gonna nail him. 'Cause if anyone's gonna go out with that girl, it's gonna be one of us.
jokes.setup: Gob wanting to hunt down Marta's alleged lover
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly recruiting Michael to catch himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Gob recruits the actual 'hermano' to hunt down the imaginary rival, with classic AD layered misunderstanding.

jokes.text: That's just the point. I mean, we're cousins. Gender has nothing to do with it. I mean, cousins can bunk together. That's why they call it 'bunkin' cousins.'
jokes.setup: George Michael arguing he should still share room with Maeby
jokes.punchline: Invents fake phrase 'bunkin' cousins' to avoid saying 'kissin' cousins'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay showing George Michael's innocent attempt to avoid the real phrase while accidentally revealing his feelings.

jokes.text: 'Kissin' cousins.' We're not kissing! That's the point!
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to avoid romantic implications
jokes.punchline: Maeby corrects him to the actual phrase, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of George Michael's discomfort with Maeby innocently making it worse.

jokes.text: I'm gonna sleep with my daughter, and you're gonna sleep with my husband.
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining new room arrangements
jokes.punchline: Innocent statement sounds incredibly inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect double entendre that sounds terrible but is technically innocent - classic AD uncomfortable phrasing.

jokes.text: No, Mother! I can blow myself.
jokes.setup: Lucille trying to help Buster blow his nose
jokes.punchline: Buster's declaration of independence sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic AD double entendre - perfectly innocent in context but absolutely filthy out of context. Iconic quotable moment.

jokes.text: I thought you had class. My brother-in-law fires you... And you move in on my girlfriend?
jokes.setup: Buster confronting Carl about having dinner with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Uses 'class' as insult rather than literal class schedule
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay callback showing Buster's attempt at confrontation with clever word choice.

jokes.text: This is purely a social call. You know, just two adults gettin' a stew on, man.
jokes.setup: Carl defending his dinner with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Returns to stew metaphor in potentially sexual context
jokes.explanation: Continues Carl's stew running gag with added sexual ambiguity that makes Buster more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I don't know what that means, but it sounds disgusting.
jokes.setup: Carl's stew euphemism
jokes.punchline: Buster's innocent but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction from Buster acknowledging the weirdness without understanding it.

jokes.text: You're replacing my mother.
jokes.setup: Buster claiming he's leaving mother for girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Lucille 2 points out he's just finding another mother figure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant psychological insight delivered as punchline - shows Lucille 2's awareness of Buster's Oedipal issues.

jokes.text: ¿Mi hermano? Ahí. There, in the green? Great, thanks. Gracias.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for 'Hermano' at soap opera set
jokes.punchline: Gets directed to different people, none of whom are the 'hermano' Gob suspects
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of hermano misunderstanding with Michael getting misdirected while looking for himself.

jokes.text: I've been dating an old lady.
jokes.setup: Buster sees Marta and is struck by her beauty
jokes.punchline: Sudden realization about his relationship with Lucille 2
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment of clarity - seeing age-appropriate woman makes him realize his pattern.

jokes.text: She's at the house. She's at the house! You're right. Right about what? She's there, what's going on? Who's with you? If it's Marta, say 'nobody.' Nobody. Well, now I don't know what's going on.
jokes.setup: Gob calling about Marta while she's with Michael
jokes.punchline: The code system immediately breaks down into confusion
jokes.explanation: Great comedy of errors with Gob's overcomplicated spy talk immediately falling apart.

jokes.text: Maybe because you've only had sex four times. Four women! Not four times!
jokes.setup: Gob saying women feel safe around Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's desperate clarification about his sexual experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - defensive about his masculinity while missing the bigger point Gob is making.

jokes.text: The competition frightened Tobias, which he felt he could use in his performance. Unfortunately this made him more confident... which frightened him again.
jokes.setup: Tobias at audition for 'Frightened Inmate #2'
jokes.punchline: Circular logic of fear making him confident making him scared again
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic perfectly capturing Tobias's neurotic overthinking, excellent structure

jokes.text: Can I let you in on a little secret? Hmm? Having him in the next room makes me miss him all the more.
jokes.setup: Maeby asking about parents' relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's fake romantic sentiment about separation
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's inability to be honest even with her daughter, creating fake romantic narrative.

jokes.text: Mom, please. He's got a big-boy crush on Action Jackson.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to romanticize the separation
jokes.punchline: Maeby's blunt assessment of her father's Carl Weathers obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby line - cuts through parental BS with childish but accurate phrasing.

jokes.text: No! We may have to bolt.
jokes.setup: Maeby starting to unpack
jokes.punchline: Lindsay still planning her midnight escape
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Lindsay hasn't given up on her dramatic escape plan.

jokes.text: What? Is she not really related to me? Is she just some girl I know?
jokes.setup: George Michael hearing about conception problems
jokes.punchline: Hopeful misunderstanding about Maeby's relation to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - George Michael's hopeful interpretation of adoption possibility for romantic reasons.

jokes.text: You could have been sleeping with 'Frightened Inmate #2.'
jokes.setup: Tobias getting the acting job
jokes.punchline: Treats minor background role as major romantic victory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias delusion - treating tiny acting role as evidence of his desirability.

jokes.text: [Narrator] And that's when Lucille realized... That she just might like being alone after all.
jokes.setup: Lucille watching George Sr.'s self-help video about being alone
jokes.punchline: She actually enjoys the solitude and can turn up TV volume
jokes.explanation: Nice character growth moment showing Lucille actually benefiting from her husband's advice.

jokes.text: I'll give you what you want... if you give me what I want. Tell me everything you know about...'Hermano.'
jokes.setup: Michael trying to get information from Marta's son
jokes.punchline: Negotiating with child to learn about himself
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony with Michael unknowingly investigating himself through a child.

jokes.text: Sí, mi hermano. He's here? Shh. He's sleeping.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about 'hermano'
jokes.punchline: Child points to sleeping actual brother while Michael misunderstands
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - audience and Michael think we're finding the rival when it's just a literal brother.

jokes.text: What does 'te amo' mean? I love you. Hmm. And who's in there? Mi hermano. Brother.
jokes.setup: Michael sees gift and asks about Spanish phrases
jokes.punchline: The revelation that 'hermano' means brother
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to entire hermano subplot - economical revelation that recontextualizes everything

jokes.text: What does 'te amo' mean? I love you. Hmm. And who's in there? Mi hermano. Brother. I've made a huge mistake.
jokes.setup: Michael finally understanding the Spanish and situation
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's the 'hermano' Gob is hunting, uses Gob's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect resolution of the hermano mystery with brilliant callback. Michael becomes what he was hunting and uses Gob's signature phrase.

jokes.text: So, you wanna go to the movies? Ow! I'll let you know when I get back from the lab.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking Maeby to movies (as test)
jokes.punchline: She hurts him and he treats it like evidence for investigation
jokes.explanation: Shows George Michael's scientific approach to his cousin crush with misguided methodology.

jokes.text: There's a shower scene? I have to be nude. Well, you don't shower with your clothes on, now, do ya?
jokes.setup: Tobias discovering his role requires nudity
jokes.punchline: Director's logical response to never-nude's obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to never-nude setup with practical consequences for his acting career.

jokes.text: Yo te quiero mucho. Yo te quiero mucho. Te amo, Marta. Te amo.
jokes.setup: Buster rebounding with Marta
jokes.punchline: Immediately professing love in Spanish he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Shows Buster's impulsive romantic nature and creates new complications for the love triangle.
